review_scope.focus: visualization overhaul for activation-based pruning presentations

diagnosed_gaps: Candidate selection is not clearly visualized at per-channel granularity.
Dependency discovery stage is mostly static and not a clear sweep narrative.
Surgery animation does not strongly communicate candidate->pruned->kept transitions.
Final before/after architecture comparison lacks strong synchronized graph storytelling.
Multiple visualization modules overlap and should be consolidated around one trace artifact.

recommended_overhaul.phase_1: Create a unified PruningTrace artifact carrying graph, clusters, per-channel scores, masks, harmonized masks, and before/after stats.
recommended_overhaul.phase_2: Build candidate-discovery graph view with metric toggle and dependency-aware color states.
recommended_overhaul.phase_3: Build animated surgery timeline: score heat -> candidate pulse -> cut -> dependency propagation -> settle.
recommended_overhaul.phase_4: Build side-by-side before/after architecture graph with synchronized layout and channel-aware node sizing.
recommended_overhaul.phase_5: Wire upgraded visuals into notebook Presentation Mode and export pipeline.
recommended_overhaul.phase_6: Add tests for visual data correctness and export integrity.
